sections.label: THINKING
sections.content: The board changed! Clicking on the white center tile (row 50, col 30) changed it from white to green (N - light green). Looking at the image, the center tile of the right grid is now green instead of white. So the mechanic is: clicking tiles in the right grid changes their color. Let me understand the pattern better and figure out the goal.

sections.label: ASSISTANT
sections.content:
World model: Clicking the center white tile in the right grid changed it to light green (N). The mechanic is clicking tiles to change their color. Need to understand the full pattern and goal.
Plan: Check what changed exactly, then try clicking other tiles to understand the color cycle and target pattern.

sections.label: TOOL CALL: python
sections.content:
<tool_call>
<function=python>
<parameter=code>
# Check what changed - compare right grid before and after
before = last_transition.before_frame.ascii.split('\n')
after = current_frame.ascii.split('\n')

print("=== Right grid diff (rows 47-63, cols 21-40) ===")
for r in range(47, 64):
    before_row = before[r][21:40]
    after_row = after[r][21:40]
    if before_row != after_row:
        print(f"Row {r} changed:")
        print(f"  Before: {before_row}")
        print(f"  After:  {after_row}")

# Check the updated segmentation for the right grid
seg = current_frame.segmentation
print("\n=== Right grid nodes ===")
for n in seg['nodes']:
    if n['id'] >= 7:  # Right grid area
        print(f"  id={n['id']} color={n['color']} pixels={n['pixels']}")
</parameter>
</function>
</tool_call>
